key_insight: Large AI models can turn solid electrolyte discovery into a closed-loop, self-improving process by linking literature knowledge, candidate design, simulation, and experiment.
engineering_relevance: This framework could speed the development of safer, higher-performance metal batteries by optimizing ion transport, stability, and mechanical robustness together.
method: Use MLIP models to bridge DFT accuracy with long-timescale ion migration simulations.
Use LLMs for literature mining, hypothesis generation, and scientific reasoning.
Integrate AI-driven design, uncertainty-aware selection, and experimental validation in a closed loop.
evidence_signal: The paper synthesizes a framework from the current state of fragmented data and multiscale challenges, emphasizing dynamic knowledge systems rather than reporting a single benchmark result.
watch_item: Autonomous discovery will still be limited by data standardization, interfacial complexity, and reproducibility across simulations and experiments.
paper_title: Breaking Bottlenecks in Solid Electrolyte Discovery with Large Artificial Intelligence Models
